seo.title: Legal Transcription Alternatives — AI Summary & Search | Dodonai
seo.description: Most legal transcription services give you text. Dodonai gives you the summary, citations, and searchable record. Compare alternatives for deposition and case prep work.

hero.headline: Legal Transcription Alternatives: From Raw Text to Case-Ready Summary
hero.subheadline: Transcription services hand you a verbatim record — and 200 pages you still have to read. Dodonai goes further: page-line summaries with citations, searchable transcripts across your case library, and topic-based reprocessing in minutes.

sections.heading: The Gap Between a Transcript and Case Prep
paragraphs: Legal transcription services solve one problem: getting from audio to accurate text. That's the easy part of the workflow. The hard part is what happens next — reading 200 pages of testimony, finding the moments that matter, building a chronology, locating the contradiction the witness already gave in a prior depo.

Most attorneys searching for transcription alternatives aren't unhappy with the transcript itself. They're frustrated that they still have to do everything else manually. The alternative they're looking for is a tool that closes the loop — turns the transcript into a summary, makes the full case library searchable, and surfaces the cross-examination angles automatically.
That's a different category of product. Transcription services finish at the text file. Dodonai starts there.

sections.heading: What Attorneys Get Beyond the Transcript

cards.title: Page-Line and Narrative Summaries
cards.description: Upload the transcript and receive a page-line summary, a narrative summary, or an issue-based summary with citations to the exact testimony. A 200-page expert depo summarized in roughly 6 minutes.

cards.title: Search Across Your Entire Case Library
cards.description: Load every transcript from the case and search by meaning, not just keywords. Find every instance an expert discussed causation across five different depositions, even when phrasing differs.

cards.title: Reprocess for New Issues Without Re-Reading
cards.description: Theory of the case changes? Run a new summary focused on different topics in minutes. Transcription services would require you to re-read manually.

cards.title: Built for Legal Workflow Security
cards.description: SOC 2 certified, HIPAA compliant, AES-256 encryption, signed BAAs, and a policy that your data is never used for AI training. Most general transcription tools weren't designed for legal data.

sections.heading: When a Transcription Service Is Still the Right Choice
paragraphs: If you only need the verbatim record for filing — certified transcripts for a court submission, sworn testimony for the record, audio-to-text for client interview archives — a dedicated transcription service or court reporter remains the right tool. Dodonai is not a certified transcription provider.

The case for Dodonai is when the transcript is the input to your real work, not the output. Deposition prep, expert review, cross-examination outlines, settlement analysis, MSJ briefing — anywhere the next step after 'we have the transcript' is 'now we have to read it,' Dodonai compresses that step from hours to minutes.

faq.question: Does Dodonai produce certified transcripts?
faq.answer: No. Dodonai is a deposition summary and analysis platform — not a certified transcription service. Upload transcripts you already have from court reporters or transcription vendors and Dodonai turns them into summaries, chronologies, and searchable testimony.

faq.question: What if my transcripts come in different formats from different court reporters?
faq.answer: Dodonai accepts PDF, Word, and text files from all major court reporting services. No preprocessing or special formatting required. Mixed-format case libraries process the same way.

faq.question: How does the cost compare to running everything through a transcription service?
faq.answer: Transcription services price per minute of audio or per page of transcript. Dodonai prices per page processed — Lite at $30/month for 200 pages, Basic at $60/month for 1,000 pages, Pro at $100/month for 5,000 pages. A typical 100-page deposition costs roughly $2 to $6 to summarize, far below per-page transcription rates because you're paying for analysis, not the underlying text.

faq.question: Can I use Dodonai alongside an existing transcription service?
faq.answer: Yes. Most firms do. The transcription service produces the certified record; Dodonai processes that record into summaries, chronologies, and a searchable testimony library. The two services complement each other rather than compete.

faq.question: What about confidentiality and AI training?
faq.answer: Your files are encrypted from upload to deletion, the platform is SOC 2 certified and HIPAA compliant, and your data is never used to train AI models. You set the retention schedule. This is materially different from general-purpose AI tools where input data may be used for model improvement.
